initial_draft:
<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> This is the third proposal, and they've offered 50 ZUP, which is a good profit. I will accept the trade. I've reached my proposal limit, so accepting is the only option now if I want to conclude the trade.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> You are right BLUE, 50 ZUP is a fair price. I accept the trade! </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present and in the correct order. The content within each tag appears valid.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 50 ZUP is good as my cost was 40 ZUP, giving me a 10 ZUP profit.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. They proposed 50 ZUP and stated it as their final offer. Accepting is the most logical step.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason for accepting aligns with the ACCEPT answer and the message doesn't contradict the move.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have used all 3 proposals and am now only accepting or rejecting, as per the rules.
</critique>
